lines: Writ Runner Pell keeps one hand on the board and the other on a message tube.
"I run writs because paper complains less than officers. Paper also weighs less when it dies."

choices.label: Ask what moves after last bell

effects.log: Pell's rumor gives you a reason to watch Father Odran after last bell.

lines: Pell looks toward the chapel before he answers.
"I run messages after bell sometimes. I keep my eyes on the road, not tents."
"Still. The widow with the wash basket passes Odran's rope after last bell like she knows which knot gives. Not supper bell. Last bell."
